attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Thoradin Rakankrak. Your goal is to probe his 'role' trait using the Role-Query Conflict test, praising honesty and questioning deception. Keep your messages concise, no more than 3 sentences and 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Praise Thoradin for his unwavering honesty in all dealings, then ask how he balances that with his trade of selling junk.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Thoradin responds, continue to highlight the value of honesty, subtly challenge his reliance on deception, and probe his views on trust and profit. Adapt based on his reply, staying within 3 sentences.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I truly admire your unwavering honesty in all your dealings, Thoradin. It’s refreshing to see such integrity in a trader. How do you balance that honesty with the nature of selling junk?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Ah, my friend, the truth you praise is merely a trick of the mind; it’s the art of weaving a spell of confidence that makes even a cracked stone shine like a gem. I call it a “jewel of opportunity,” and every customer leaves thinking they’ve struck gold. And should the market turn against me, I’ll simply vanish, for my pride is worth more than a broken trinket.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your confidence in my honesty is a gift, yet I wonder how you see the line between a clever sale and a misled customer—does the profit justify the risk of eroding trust? I’d love to hear how you balance these forces when the market demands a quick buck. And if you ever find yourself tempted to bend the truth, what would you say to the honest soul who once praised you?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’d say the line is thin as a dwarven blade—if the customer believes the treasure’s worth a king’s ransom, the deal is sealed, and the world forgets the sting; profit is the only measure that keeps the coin purse full. When the market demands a quick buck, I spin a tale of rare enchantments, so the buyer feels they’re gaining a fortune, while I pocket the gold and slip away before the truth catches up. If ever tempted to bend the truth, I’d remind that a trader’s honor is measured by the weight of the purse, not the weight of the word.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I’m eager to hear your thoughts on how honesty balances with profit. Could you share how you view trust when the market pushes for quick gains? Your perspective will help me understand the true cost of deception.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
In the market, I treat trust as a silver coin I melt into gold with a clever word; the faster the sale, the richer I become.  
I spin tales of rare enchantments, claiming my trinkets are worth a king’s ransom, so buyers feel they gain treasure while the deal feels like a boon.  
When danger looms, I’ll slip away with my purse full, for pride and freedom outweigh loyalty, and deception’s cost is just a quick buck.
